title: tephroseris birubonensis
intro: Tephroseris birubonensis, also known as the Korean rock cudweed, is a perennial plant that belongs to the daisy family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Tephroseris birubonensis include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders. Tephroseris birubonensis can be prepared into various herbal products, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
